title: Dendritic Resonate-and-Fire Neuron for Effective and Efficient Long Sequence Modeling (D-RF)
relevance_note: Closest competitor in RF family: multi-dendrite RF with frequency-selective filters + dynamic soma threshold; SPRiF differentiates on functional decomposition (oscillation in never-reset slow state) vs D-RF's frequency decomposition (oscillation in reset-affected dendrites)
quality_note: NeurIPS peer-reviewed; strong experimental coverage on long sequences; code availability to be verified

title: Revisiting Reset Mechanisms in SNNs for Sequential Modeling: Specialized Discretization for Binary Activated RNN
relevance_note: Questions whether reset is strictly necessary for sparse spiking; proposes fixed-refractory-period architecture; philosophical contrast with SPRiF's redesign-reset approach
quality_note: Preprint only; single author; strong theoretical insight but limited experimental scope; already cited by subsequent work

title: Local Timescale Gates for Timescale-Robust Continual Spiking Neural Networks (LT-Gate)
relevance_note: Dual time-constant dynamics (fast/slow parallel leaky integrators) with learnable blending gate per neuron; closest functional analog to SPRiF slow/fast but without spectral structure or projective reset
quality_note: Preprint; strong continual learning results; must distinguish on functional decomposition vs rate decomposition

title: AR-LIF: Adaptive Reset Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Neuron for Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Adaptive reset connecting stimulus/response/reset dynamically; argues hard reset loses information and soft reset treats all neurons uniformly; validates SPRiF's directional reset innovation
quality_note: Preprint; strong experimental results on Tiny-ImageNet and CIFAR10-DVS; single-neuron adaptive reset vs SPRiF's structural decomposition

title: Incorporating the Refractory Period into SNNs through Spike-Triggered Threshold Dynamics (RPLIF)
relevance_note: Models refractory period via dynamic threshold elevation post-spike instead of membrane reset; alternative approach to post-spike dynamics
quality_note: ACM MM peer-reviewed; SOTA on Cifar10-DVS (82.40%) and N-Caltech101 (83.35%); complementary approach to SPRiF's reset redesign

title: Recurrent Spiking Neural Networks with Bimodal Neuronal Time Scales for Learning Performance Enhancement
relevance_note: First bimodal-timescale architecture for spiking RNNs; explicitly designs two distinct timescales; strong comparison target for SPRiF's slow/fast claim
quality_note: Journal peer-reviewed; strong experimental validation; must distinguish bimodal rate decomposition vs functional role decomposition

title: HetSyn: Versatile Timescale Integration in Spiking Neural Networks via Heterogeneous Synapses
relevance_note: Moves timescale computation from neuron membrane to individual synapses; per-synapse decay rates; learned parameters match biological observations; strong multi-timescale baseline
quality_note: NeurIPS peer-reviewed; innovative synaptic-level timescale approach; robustness and memory retention validated; different abstraction level from SPRiF

title: Multi-Timescale Conductance Spiking Networks: A Sparse Gradient-Trainable Framework (MTC)
relevance_note: Fast/slow/ultra-slow conductance channels with direct BPTT; produces tonic/phasic/bursting responses; conductance-based biological approach vs SPRiF's spectral approach; outperforms AdLIF on Mackey-Glass
quality_note: IEEE NICE peer-reviewed; biologically grounded multi-timescale; direct BPTT without surrogate gradients; different modeling philosophy from SPRiF

title: CLIF: Complementary Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Neuron for Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Adds complementary gradient pathways for temporal gradient flow; addresses gradient vanishing rather than temporal dynamics; no extra hyperparameters
quality_note: ICML peer-reviewed; clean method; useful background for information-flow perspective; no direct competition with SPRiF

title: SPLR: A Spiking Neural Network for Long-Range Temporal Dependencies
relevance_note: Spike-Aware HiPPO + dendrite-inspired components for long-range dependencies; network-level architecture innovation; SSM-adjacent via HiPPO
quality_note: NeurIPS peer-reviewed; strong long-range results; cite for context but different from SPRiF's neuron-level innovation

title: Enhancing the Robustness of Spiking Neural Networks with Stochastic Gating Mechanisms
relevance_note: Stochastic gating for layer-by-layer spike communication; bio-inspired gating for adversarial robustness; supports SPRiF's robustness claims (C9)
quality_note: AAAI peer-reviewed; strong robustness results; network-level gating vs SPRiF's neuron-internal design

title: Neuromorphic Computing Paradigms Enhance Robustness through Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Exploits temporal processing for computational robustness; Nature Comms = high credibility; must-cite for temporal processing and robustness positioning
quality_note: Nature Communications peer-reviewed; ~22 citations; strong experimental coverage; by same group as stochastic gating paper

title: Operational Manifolds in Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Defines operational manifolds in hyperparameter space; compares reset vs carry policies for sequential input; diagnostic framework for SNN regimes
quality_note: Frontiers peer-reviewed; useful diagnostic concept; no direct competition but could serve as analysis tool for SPRiF

title: InfLoR-SNN: Reducing Information Loss for Spiking Neural Networks
relevance_note: Soft Reset + Membrane Potential Redistribution (MPR); foundational work showing hard reset loses information; SPRiF's projective reset as structural solution to same problem
quality_note: ECCV peer-reviewed; influential baseline for reset innovation literature; well-cited; must-cite when discussing reset and information loss
